entry.title: Re: Manual status Change the CHARM document
entry.content:
Usually when the transport is "ahead" that should not impact setting the status with either the action or the report CRM_SOCM_SERVICE_REPORT. If it doesn't work with this report, then there's probably another problem at the same time (RC=8 on the transport or something completely different).

For tough cases we had a developper write us a Z-report, but for single incidents debugging will also do.
With both approaches, keep in mind that you not only have to set the user Status Exxxx but also the system status Ixxxx to avoid problems further down the road.

entry.title: Onboarding 2.0 – Duplicate Name Scenario / Rehire Verification
entry.content:
Hi all,

This is related to Onboarding 2.0.

We encountered a scenario that we haven’t come across before.

There is an inactive profile in the system with:

  • First Name: ABC
  • Last Name: XYZ

We initiated onboarding for a new external candidate from the Recruitment module who has the same first and last name, but different TFN, DOB, and email address.

During onboarding initiation from RCM, the "Pre-Onboarding Rehire Verification" step was triggered. I selected "Proceed to New Hire".

However, after completing the Personal Data step in Onboarding, the "Onboarding Rehire Verification" step was triggered again.

In Recruit/Rehire Configuration → MatchingUserConfiguration (MATCHINGUSERCONFIG), we have certain matching criteria maintained.

Could you please confirm:

  • Is the rehire verification step being triggered due to the configuration in MATCHINGUSERCONFIG?
  • If so, which specific matching criteria (e.g., First Name / Last Name) is causing this duplicate rehire check?

     

    Thanks,

    Rohini

entry.title: Re: Account Assignment Category Q not available PR item l...
entry.content:
Hello @kishore_kumar29,

as Lakshmipathi  said, Account Assignment Q is not available in S/4 HANA Public Cloud standard.

 

We have achieved the usage by working with two BADIs:

For a scenario a list of predefined conditions is delivered. In case you need to extend this list, you can extend the condition list with additional conditions (BAdI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF).

In SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL you must evaluate the values of appended conditions. By it you have the control that dependent on your condition process instances will be started or steps will be created.

 

1️⃣   SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF: for Providing additional conditions for scenarios

 

2️⃣   SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL: for Value evaluation of additional conditions for scenarios

 

3️⃣   Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions: Result can be seen and set up here

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: "Error in entity 'ZBRAND1_ITEM_PROJ(CDS)': Draft is not supported" while publishing a service in RAP
entry.content:
We are attempting to extend the transactional view R_SourcingProjectTP by adding additional fields via an extension. As part of this, we created a custom projection view and exposed it through a new service. However, during service publishing, we encounter the following error:

Error: "Error in entity 'ZBRAND1_ITEM_PROJ(CDS)': Draft is not supported"

Steps performed:

  1. Extension of Standard Interface View
    • Created an extension on the standard interface view R_SourcingProjectTP, which is draft-enabled.
  2. Behavior Extension
    • Extended the behavior definition for the same interface view.
  3. Child Projection Creation
    • Created a projection view on the child entity R_SourcingProjectItemTP.
  4. Custom Projection View (Parent)
    • Created a custom projection view on top of R_SourcingProjectTP.
    • Added an association to the custom projection of R_SourcingProjectItemTP.
    • Maintained proper redirection to the child entity.
  5. Behavior Projection Definitions
    • For the parent projection:

ABAP

use association _SourcingProjectItem { create; with draft; }

Show more lines

    • For the child projection:

ABAP

define behavior for zitem_projection

{

use update;

use delete;

use association _SourcingProject { with draft; }

}

 

Show more lines

  1. Metadata Extension
    • Extended metadata for both parent and child projection views.
  2. Service Definition
    • Created and exposed both projection views in a service.

Issue

While publishing the service, the activation fails with the error:

"Draft is not supported" for entity ZBRAND1_ITEM_PROJ.

 

We would appreciate any guidance or suggestions on resolving this issue and correctly enabling draft support in this scenario.

entry.content:
Add MSEG-PRCTR in the Customizing : SPRO -> Material Management -> Inventory Management and Physical Inventory -> Reporting -> Define Field Selection for Material Document List

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: SAP Sapphire 2026 Special Edition
entry.content:
This week's edition of the SAP Community Voice celebrates one of our most waited and biggest events: SAP Sapphire 2026!  

The event that happened in Orlando and now in Madrid, gave the opportunity for attendees to learn about Joule’s new central workspace, experience live demos and see how Joule proactively improves operations. The highlights for our SAP community was also to connect with experts, partners, and peers to talk about AI strategies and insights about SAP new announcements.  

If you had the chance to be there in person or watch the keynotes virtually, you probably are excited about the new SAP Business AI Platform, which forms the basis for the future of business: the Autonomous Enterprise, where agents run the business and you can focus on what matters.  

And our team believes there’s no better place to start than by investing time in learning. Today, we are excited to share more details about some of the recent announcements that could help you reach the next level.

Check out now:

  • SAP Certification Turns 30 and Expands Its Portfolio for the AI Era:  

A transformed certification approach and new AI-assisted learning capabilities in SAP Learning Hub redefine how SAP professionals build, prove, and maintain their skills as SAP Certification turns 30 in 2026, marking both a milestone and the completion of the most significant transformation in the program's history. Building on the new performance-based assessment approach introduced at SAP TechEd 2025, the full SAP Certification portfolio of more than 100 certifications now moves away from traditional multiple-choice formats to hands-on practical assessments, while candidates can leverage AI tools during exams, mirroring the tools and resources they use every day. This is part of SAP's commitment to equipping 12 million people with AI-ready skills by 2030), building on the more than 4 million people already reached in 2025. 

 

  • Practical Assessments Across the Full Portfolio 

The assessment framework includes role-plays, challenge-based case studies, and practical tasks that mirror actual scenarios professionals encounter in SAP implementations, while system-based exams have Joule for Consultants built in and candidates can use AI support and documentation throughout, mirroring the tools and resources they rely on every day. Each certification includes comprehensive learning journeys and practical exercises, and more than 100,000 certification exams have already been taken under the new model. According to CIO magazine, SAP is among the first major enterprise software vendors to implement this shift at scale.  

Available globally now at https://learning.sap.com/get-certified/reimagining-certification.

 

  • SAP Learning Hub Introduces AI-Assisted Learning Capabilities 

Alongside the certification transformation, SAP Learning Hub, the single destination for customers and partners to learn, practice, and prove their SAP skills, is introducing new AI-assisted capabilities in its customer and partner editions. Already embraced by over 7,500 early users, the new capabilities allow learners to engage with SAP course content beyond traditional linear learning by asking questions and getting instant answers in over 80 languages, with precise citations from SAP's trusted learning content, while additional AI-generated formats include podcasts, interactive sessions with AI hosts, automatically generated FAQs, mind maps, study guides, quizzes, and video overviews. The business impact is measurable: LTIMindtree reported 50 percent faster onboarding, and for Dieter Schoon, Chief People Officer at NTT Data Business Solutions, SAP Learning Hub has become the primary way their talent becomes future-ready in the agentic AI era. 

 Available globally now at https://learning.sap.com/learninghub-subscriptions.

 

  • SAP introduces new learning journey, certification for data architects 

As AI becomes embedded across enterprise operations, the quality of the data it relies on can be a competitive differentiator. SAP is introducing “Becoming a Certified SAP Data Architect,” a new learning journey that includes a certification in SAP Business Data Cloud, designed to help data architects build the data foundation AI requires. The learning journey addresses a key challenge for enterprise AI (data fragmented across disconnected systems) by helping learners develop skills to connect, manage, and govern data across the enterprise using SAP Business Data Cloud, ensuring AI models can access consistent and reliable information while enabling teams to move faster from raw data to business decisions. Data architects who complete the learning journey and earn the certification will be prepared to support a data fabric approach, so data stays coherent and accessible across the enterprise. The learning journey is now available on the SAP Learning site.

 

  • SAP Expands Access to AI-Ready Skills with New Learning Offerings and Accenture Partnership 

As the AI-ready skills gap becomes a defining challenge for organizations worldwide, with the World Economic Forum identifying skills shortages as the primary barrier to business transformation ahead of investment constraints and regulatory complexity, SAP is expanding its learning portfolio with new offerings designed to increase access, relevance, and scalability of AI-ready skills development, supporting its commitment to equip 12 million people with AI-ready skills by 2030 (Read more details here).

 

  • Accenture Becomes First Global Authorized Partner to Deliver SAP Learning Class 

SAP is introducing a partner-led delivery model for SAP Learning Class, its premium instructor-led training offering, with Accenture becoming the first SAP Authorized Education Partner and SAP Global Strategic Services Partner to deliver these courses worldwide. The model combines SAP-authored content and pre-configured SAP training systems with Accenture's global delivery capabilities, industry expertise, and local accessibility across time zones and geographies, while Accenture instructors bring practical insight into how SAP integrates within complete enterprise technology stacks, including SAP's AI capabilities. SAP Learning Class can also be combined with self-paced learning on the SAP Learning site, creating a continuous development path from foundational knowledge to hands-on experience and SAP Certification. 

Available globally now at SAP Training Shop.

 

  • SAP Makes Selected AI Live Sessions Available to All Learners 

SAP is offering free AI-focused live sessions open to everyone, covering Business AI topics across awareness, adoption, and demonstrations: 

  • Accelerate your transformation to the Cloud with SAP Business AI 
  • Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI 
  • All Things Business AI: Latest Insights, Updates & Live Demos 
  • Build AI Securely: Avoid Pitfalls in the Development and Operations Lifecycle 

Available now at https://learning.sap.com/artificial-intelligence.

 

  • New Learning Journey for Enterprise Architects Leading AI Transformation 

Enterprise architects play a central role in shaping AI adoption and transformation strategies, yet role-specific training for this audience has been largely absent from the market. The new learning journey, “Leading AI-Driven Transformation as an SAP Enterprise Architect,” addresses this gap by providing frameworks and guidance on aligning AI initiatives with business objectives, assessing organizational readiness, managing architectural risk, and designing scalable, secure enterprise landscapes. 

Available globally from May 2026, free of charge at SAP Learning site.

 

  • Clean Core: Building the Foundation for Innovation and AI Readiness 

A standardized and upgrade-stable SAP S/4HANA core enables organizations to adopt new capabilities more quickly, reduce upgrade cycles from months to days, and free resources for innovation, while also creating the data foundation AI requires through standardized data models and reduced system fragmentation that allow AI solutions to access consistent, enterprise-wide data. The course “Clean Core: Applying Clean Core Principles in SAP S/4HANA” provides guidance on governance, architecture, and cloud-compliant extensions for developers, consultants, and architects responsible for building future-ready SAP environments. 

Available globally now, free of charge 2026 at SAP Learning site 

For more details, please check the SAP Sapphire Innovation News Guide 2026  

SAP Community at SAP Sapphire 2026  

Beyond the amazing news, for our SAP community the event also becomes a wonderful opportunity to get insight from the experts, network with professionals from all over the world, learn more details about all sorts of SAP topics and meet other members. This year we were very proud to have the SAP Mentors and the SAP Champions (@david_ruizb , @Dominik_Tylczynski , @tonjakehrer, @sumikko) in the event. In case you also attended the event in Orlando, Madrid or even virtually, we would like to encourage you to write a blog post telling your opinions, insights and thoughts about the event. You can share the content inside the Technology Blog Post, in the Community Corner, or any other group or topic of your preference (check here the list of all interest groups). The idea is to give you an open space to share what you think about SAP new vision for how business will run from now on.

entry.title: CSapEditiorCtrl::GetObject: Object 164 does not exist in SAP GUI 800.
entry.content: I encounter the error 'CSapEditiorCtrl::GetObject: Object 164 does not exist' when trying to edit text in Smart Forms while using SAP GUI 800. This issue started after upgrading to SAP GUI 740 Patch 5 or newer. It is caused by the removal of the legacy SAPscript Text Editor controls from the SAP GUI, replaced by the MS Word editor. The error suggests a missing control object issue, likely related to the missing legacy editor controls that are no longer included by default. To resolve this, I need to either reinstall the SAPscript Legacy Text Editor component from SAP or properly configure MS Word as the editor, ensuring the correct settings are applied to avoid this error.

entry.title: Re: Drop documents here. If you drag multiple files, pick...
entry.content:
The message is correct.

You can drop files from your computer into the drop area. For example, on a Windows system, you can drag files from your desktop or from the file explorer and drop them into the drop area of Solution Documentation. When you have dropped files to upload in the drop area, the Upload Documents dialog opens. More details can be found in Drag and Drop of Documents | SAP Help Portal.

If uploading documents fails, you can create a case under the component SV-SMG-IMP for further check.

entry.content:
Hi Mahil,

You don't need a BAdI or User Exit for this. If you set procedure PPAU (Serial numbers in PP order) and PPRL (PP order release) to 04 (Automatic) in the serial number profile customizing, serial numbers get automatically created when the production order is saved.

Here's the corresponding SAP Help documentation for On Premise/Private Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/25a41481f62e469ba0e61015a0d39d20/db0eb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

Alex

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to disable document splitting in 2602
entry.content:
Hi AIDream,

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, document splitting is always active and cannot be deactivated.
However, you can use the Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting (ID: 102322) configuration activity to deactivate the profit center as a mandatory splitting characteristic. This means that the profit center is no longer required during posting, meaning it does not have to be entered. As a result, documents can exist in the system where the profit center is not filled.
Nevertheless, the profit center remains an active splitting characteristic. This means that if a profit center is provided, document splitting will still be carried out based on it and values will be inherited accordingly, and a default value will be applied if one is defined.

Best regards,
Verena

entry.title: IFRS 18 Compliance: Generating Comparative P&L Reports from SAP Cloud ERP, Private and SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Introduction 

IFRS 18 changes how companies show their financial results by making things clearer and more consistent. It groups income and expenses in a standard way, defines clear totals like operating profit, and requires companies to explain any extra performance measures they use. This helps people better understand and compare how companies are doing. 

One of the toughest challenges with IFRS 18 is reworking past financial results so they match the new reporting format, including shifting items, adjusting, and ensuring everything is presented consistently year over year. 

This blog examines how organizations operating on SAP Cloud ERP & SAP Cloud ERP, Private can effectively and pragmatically meet IFRS 18 comparative profit and loss (P&L) reporting requirements. It focuses on approaches that minimize disruption to existing reporting processes while ensuring compliance, leveraging standard capabilities and targeted enhancements rather than large-scale system overhauls. 

How SAP Cloud ERP, Private and SAP Cloud ERP Support IFRS 18 Classification 

As a first step toward compliance, you need to identify how your current GL account structure aligns with the IFRS 18 categories — Operating, Investing, and Financing. SAP Cloud ERP, Private and SAP Cloud ERP provide two main approaches, based on how much detail is needed. 

  1. GL Account-Level Classification (Financial Statement Version (FSV) / Manage Global Hierarchy) 

If your business can clearly group existing GL accounts into IFRS 18 categories, you can use the following: 

  • Financial Statement Versions (FSV): This lets you create a hierarchy of GL accounts that matches the IFRS 18 structure, including the required subtotals. 
  • Manage Global Hierarchy app: This is another option instead of FSV. It offers more flexibility in creating hierarchies and supports functional areas for IFRS 18 reporting.

This is the easier approach. It works well for organizations where the level of detail in GL accounts is enough to separate operating, investing, and financing activities. 

    2. Transaction-Level Classification using Functional Area 

In some cases, a single GL account may include different types of transactions, such as both operating and investing activities. To handle this, you can use the Functional Area field to classify transactions at the time of posting. This helps assign the correct IFRS 18 category more accurately. 

Note: If your organization is already using the Functional Area field for other purposes (for example, cost of sales reporting), then using it again for IFRS 18 classification could create conflicts. In such situations, you can use another approach. You can create a custom field using Custom Fields  app, where you define your own rules to classify each transaction as operating, investing, or financing. 

SAP provides standard Fiori apps to help generate financial statements that comply with IFRS 18: 

 

Fiori App 

Use Case 

Balance Sheet / Income Statement 

IFRS 18 reporting at the GL account level 

W0161 – Balance Sheet / Income Statement (Multidimensional) 

Reporting that combines GL account and Functional Area 

These apps integrate with Financial Statement Version (FSV) and Global Hierarchy to render IFRS 18-compliant P&L presentations including the required subtotals. 

The Comparative Reporting Challenge 

IFRS 18 will take effect from 1 January 2027, so 2026 will be the year used for comparison. How easy this comparison is will depend on what the organization does during 2026. 

Scenario 1: Steps Taken in 2026 (Proactive Adoption) 

Organizations that have already started recording transactions as per IFRS 18 requirements in 2026—whether using their general ledger (GL), functional areas, or any custom setup—will already have the required data in their system. This means they can easily use the Fiori apps mentioned above to create financial statements that meet compliance requirements, including comparisons with previous periods. 

Scenario 2: No Steps Taken in 2026 (Retrospective Approach from 2027) 

Organizations that have not implemented IFRS 18 classification by 2026 will need to restate the previous year’s financial data for comparison. The way this is done depends on how the organization manages its classifications. 

For organizations using General Ledger (GL) account-based reporting: 

Since changes cannot be made directly in audited financial records, the recommended approach is to: 

  1. Create an Extension Ledger in SAP .
  2. Record IFRS 18 reclassification or adjustment entries in this extension ledger 
  3. Use SAP Fiori apps to generate the comparative Profit & Loss (P&L) statement from the extension ledger 

This approach keeps the original audited financial records unchanged while allowing the organization to present financial statements that comply with IFRS 18 for comparison purposes. 

Summary: Decision Tree for IFRS 18 Comparative Reporting 

Are your existing GL accounts sufficient to classify into Operating / Investing / Financing? 

1.) YES → Use FSV or Manage Global Hierarchy 

         i) Report via Balance Sheet/Income Statement Fiori app for SAP Cloud ERP, Private 

         ii) Financial Statement Review Booklet in SAP Cloud ERP 

2.) NO  → Do you use Functional Area for other purposes? 

           i.) NO  → Activate Functional Area for IFRS 18 derivation 

               a.)  Report via W0161 Multidimensional Fiori app in SAP Cloud ERP, Private 

               b.) Financial Statement Review Booklet in SAP Cloud ERP 

          ii.). YES → Create Custom Dimension with derivation rules 

               a.)  Report via Fiori App.

For Comparative Year (2026) — data not captured in 2026? 

→ Create Extension Ledger → Post adjustments → Report from Extension Ledger 

Key SAP References 

SAP Note 3696338 — Advisory Note on IFRS 18 Transition in S/4HANA Private Cloud / S/4HANA 

SAP Note 3694359 - How to Adopt IFRS 18 for Financial Reporting in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Conclusion 

IFRS 18 can be managed in SAP S/4HANA using tools that already exist, such as Financial Statement Version, Global Hierarchy, Functional Area, Custom Dimensions, and Extension Ledgers. The important thing is to start early. Companies that update their general ledger structure or classify transactions properly in 2026 will find it much easier to create comparable profit and loss statements in 2027. 

If companies start late, they can still meet requirements by using an Extension Ledger. However, this method needs careful planning and proper coordination with auditors to make sure all adjustment entries are correct.

entry.title: Norway: Auto population of Payment Reference in Billing Document (KIDNO) - S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are undertaking a template roll out to Norway. There is a business requirement to auto populate the payment reference field in outgoing invoices. We are working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and based on the standard functionality I cannot see any way to auto generate the KIDNO and auto populate the payment reference field.  This is a key requirement in the Nordic countries - we will have the same issue in Sweden and Finland so finding a resolution to this issue is key.

The KID number (kundeidentifikasjon) in Norway is an abbreviation for "Customer identification". It is used to associate payments with the right customer and the right invoice. In short, the KID number is a Payment reference number. This reference is used when the customer makes electronic payments and therefore this reference appears in the bank statement and is used for clearing.

Can anyone please provide any guidance on how this can be achieved? The current ERP system which SAP is replacing auto generates this reference number.  This reference number also appears on the physical invoice & in any einvoice generated.

Regards

Claire

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Localization  SD Sales

entry.title: Re: Criar Ordem de fornecimento gratuito via API
entry.content:
Good day @Gabriel_Araujo98 

Thank you for your question.

Considering you are using Public Cloud, you can create the sales order via API, but the Brazil Additional Fiscal Data item fields “Business Transaction” and “Customer Usager” are not necessarily exposed for create, read, or update in the standard Sales Order API. Note that Customer Usage is available through Additional Fiscal Data button at sales order item level, and it represents the usage of the material by the customer. You can also configure additional Fiscal Data values you can use to provide extra information you may need for tax calculation.

What that basically means is that they relevant for tax and fiscal processing, but they can only be filled through Sales API if they are exposed in the API metadata. The standard Sales Order A2X API, API_SALES_ORDER_SRV, supports sales order create, read, update, and delete from an external system but only for the fields available in the service metadata.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/00d244581efca007e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Here is the recommended steps you should consider:

  1. Check th metadata of API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
  2. Search for Business Transaction and Customer Usage at item level.
  3. If the fields are not exposed, you cannot pass them in the API payload.
  4. Configure Brazil Additional Fiscal Data and Usage Purposes for Sales so the fields are automatically determined where it is allowed.
  5. Create the sales order by API and verify the item level Additional Fiscal Data in Manage Sales Orders.
  6. If the field must be filled externally and are not exposed, you may consider raising and influence via Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Asymmetric Reporting Layout
entry.content:
Hi all,
Unfortunately, this solution reveals a significant struggle for SAC. It forces the use of restricted and calculated measures.
In high-volume scenarios, I can see that it does not work because the system will crash during the intermediate calculations. Even if you push the filters into the measures, it becomes high-maintenance and tedious, and it can still crash.
Today, you can have a lot more data visible simply via Advanced filter + Version + 1 measure. This is not the case with restricted and calculated measures (the SAC engine was designed to have as few measures as possible).
I still don't get why we couldn't implement this directly in the version dimension or via an advanced filter. My only guess is that SAP tried to make it flexible, but at the cost of an architectural decision and report design. Many companies try to simplify for quick input by using only versions + 1 measure, rather than many measures (especially due to intermediate calculation issues and maintenance).
In real-world use cases, we need to change versions, and while input controls seem flexible, the SAC architecture suffers from performance issues and kills any calendar task that sends the versions to the layout.
An obvious limitation of this is that if you want to do Actuals Year PYTD + Actuals Month CY, you will need 3 measures to have a simple total. Another is currency conversion: to have Local and Group, simply because you cannot change the measure on the restriction dynamically, which leads to a calculation measure on top to do a simple IF, or we need to multiply everything by 2 or use JavaScript to dynamically change it.
Lastly, styling rules should have been considered because, with multiple granularities, this forces multiple rules, which will end up replicating the measures definition/logic.
BR,
Miguel

entry.title: Re: Updating Stock Type / System Status in Material Seria...
entry.content:
Dear @edoymaner,

In Public Cloud Stock Type and System Status of a material serial number should not be treated as normal master-data fields that can be directly cleared or overwritten through the MaterialSerialNumber PATCH API

The MaterialSerialNumber API is released for creating, reading and updating material serial numbers but SAP serial number design treats stock-related data as inventory/status information driven by logistics processes, not as freely editable API fields. SAP confirms that fields such as plant and storage location are filled only while the serial number is in warehouse stock with status ESTO and they are cleared by the system when the serial number leaves stock. SAP also states that in status ESTO these fields cannot be edited directly

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f9ba73d1b8c543c2bd2ba1666271af86/b6116dbbcff74afd851343ca65c54d3c.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2980219

https://api.sap.com/api/API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV/overview

Therefore:

1. Stock Type should be changed through a business transaction for example goods receipt, goods issue, transfer posting, stock transfer, blocked/unrestricted/quality stock movement, or warehouse stock type change. For Inventory Management postings the relevant released interface to evaluate is Material Documents Read, Create, which supports serial number information for material document processing

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/842c673978bd46d4874e8b9121743b70.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3f57e7df4a114edabffe8b2d581a59ed/d4c919581bc30a02e10000000a44147b.html

2. If embedded EWM / warehouse physical stock is involved, SAP provides warehouse stock APIs for stock type changes. SAP specifically documents an operation to change warehouse physical stock type for a product with serial number requirements using POST on WarehousePhysicalStockProducts. This is the right direction for warehouse stock type changes, not a direct update of the serial number master record

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/1e80073e617f4636b62b409574bc70e6.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/3e2c1d2ac8914af6b4a9a6accf426db2.html

3. System Status should not be manipulated directly by API. It is process-controlled. In correction scenarios, SAP’s documented approach is to check the serial number status in the Display Material Serial Number app and use special serial number functions / manual transaction, rather than a direct status-update API

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3400072

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Do I need to active 1SG, if I want to post a inter-co...
entry.content:
Hello @AIDream 

Thank you for your question.

You do not need to activate 1SG just to post a cross-company journal entry. 1SG is related to Group Reporting content, not the basic clearing account setup required for cross company code postings you require.

The error message F5730 you see means that clearing accounts are missing for the affected company code pair. In this case, you should maintain the intercompany clearing account determination for EVXA and EVGZ in Automatic Account Determination, SSCUI 100297, using transaction key BUV, Clearing between company codes.

Here are references for more guiding details.

3378120 - Error message no. F5730 "No accts maintained for company code clearing between comp.cds & and &." - SAP S/4HANA Cloud - SAP for Me

3678615 - Error message: F5730 "No accts maintained for company code clearing between comp.cds 1AAA and 1BBB" - SAP for Me

3673605 - Duplicate postings after activating the SSCUI 107009 'Intercompany Usage of General Journal Entries functionality' - SAP for Me

Here is what i recommend:

  1. Open Automatic Account Determination, SSCUI 100297.
  2. Search for transaction key BUV.
  3. Maintain the clearing accounts for company code pair EVXA/EVGZ.
  4. Maintain the reverse direction too if your postings can run both ways.
  5. Save and transport or publish the configuration.
  6. Repost or simulate the cross-company journal entry.
  7. Confirm that the system generates clearing lines and each company code balances to zero.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: One PO with Multiple GRNs and Multiple Invoices – How Does It Work?
entry.content:
I have a scenario where a single PO has been issued for more than 100 items, and the goods will be received in multiple batches at different times. As per the agreed payment terms, the supplier will raise 3 invoices against the same PO:

  • First invoice upon partial delivery
  • Second invoice upon subsequent delivery
  • Final invoice after complete delivery

I would like to understand the following:

  1. How can I track, for a single supplier and PO, the number of invoices raised, their payment status, and whether they are fully or partially paid?
  2. Since the payment terms require payment in 3 phases of 33.33% each, how will the payment process be managed in SAP?
  3. Will the payments be strictly linked to the quantity/value of items received through GR, or is it possible to make payments that are higher or lower than the actual goods received?
  4. In cases where payment needs to be made in advance or differs from the received quantity/value, how can this be handled in the system?

entry.title: SAP EHS WWI: change generated PDF filename from MATNR to customer code
entry.content:
We are working on an SAP EHS / WWI project related to Compliance and SDS generation.

We generate reports from transaction CG54 and use the option:

"Send to SAP spooler only for now"

The generated PDF finally appears in SAP Office with a filename following this standard pattern:

Material_<REPORT_CATEGORY>.PDF

Example:
123456_MSDS_EN.PDF

Our requirement is to replace the MATNR with a customer code / customer material number.

So far we have investigated the following:

  • WWI templates and symbols
  • Generation Variants (SRGV)
  • Report Shipping / MSDS_DIST
  • DDOC logs in SLG1
  • Programs RC1WWIDP and RC1WWIWP
  • Function module C1DA_REP_WORK_PROCESS
  • FM CVEA_RDOS_CREATE_BY_EHS

We also identified that the generated document is stored in DMS using:

C14S_DOCUMENT_ORIGINAL_PUT

and that the WWI server returns the filename in variable:

V_TRG_DOC1_NAME

through FM:

WWI_GENERATE_AND_IMPORT2

However, we are still unsure whether:

  1. The attachment filename is generated directly by the WWI server
  2. The filename is reconstructed later during DDOC / MSDS_DIST / SAPoffice processing
  3. There is any standard enhancement/BAdI/customizing specifically intended to control the attachment filename

Questions:

  • Has anyone changed the standard WWI/EHS attachment filename successfully?
  • Is there any standard BAdI, User Exit or Enhancement Spot for this?
  • Does SAP standard always generate filenames based on MATNR + Report Category + Language?
  • Is the SAPoffice attachment name taken from DMS original filename or rebuilt later during report shipping?

We are debugging standard programs/functions but we would like to avoid modifying SAP standard if a proper enhancement spot already exists.

Any hints about the correct enhancement point would be greatly appreciated.

Regards.

AG

entry.title: Re: Regarding SAP GRC
entry.content: SAP GRC is a great career choice, especially with your background in Computer Science and AI/ML. I would recommend starting with core concepts like SAP Authorization, Access Control, Risk Analysis, SoD (Segregation of Duties), Identity Access Management, and audit/compliance processes. Learning SAP S/4HANA security basics alongside SAP GRC Access Control can also be very valuable for entry-level roles. SAP Learning Hub, openSAP, and hands-on practice in SAP systems are excellent starting points. The demand for SAP GRC professionals continues to grow as organizations focus more on security, governance, and regulatory compliance. Wishing you the best on your SAP journey.

entry.title: Python Script to Create an Employee Table in a Cloud Database
entry.content:
Introduction:
Creating an employee table in a Cloud PostgreSQL database using Python helps in organizing and storing employee information in a structured way. By using libraries like psycopg2, Python can easily connect to the database and execute SQL commands. This method is commonly used in modern applications that rely on cloud-based data storage and backend systems.

Body:

Write python code to create employee table as below

 

import psycopg2
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

# Load environment variables from .env file
load_dotenv(dotenv_path='../.env')

def create_employee_table():
    try:
        # Connect to the PostgreSQL database
        conn = psycopg2.connect(os.getenv('DB_URL'))
        cur = conn.cursor()

        # SQL to create employee table
        create_table_sql = """
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS employee (
            id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
            name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
            gender VARCHAR(2),
            salary DECIMAL(10, 2),
            currency VARCHAR(3)
        );
        """

        # Execute the SQL
        cur.execute(create_table_sql)
        conn.commit()

        print("Employee table created successfully!")

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating table: {e}")

    finally:
        if 'cur' in locals():
            cur.close()
        if 'conn' in locals():
            conn.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    create_employee_table()

Execute the file in terminal as follows

 

 

 

Check if the table created in PostgreSQL admin console

 

 

Summary:
To achieve this, you first connect to the Cloud PostgreSQL database using Python with the required credentials. Then, you execute a SQL statement to create the employee table with necessary columns. Once the table is created, it can be used to securely store and manage employee data.

entry.title: SAP-to-Legacy Integration Using GUI_DOWNLOAD in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Introduction:

In many SAP projects, data needs to be shared with external or legacy systems for business operations. One common way to transfer data from SAP is by downloading it into a text file. 

In SAP ABAP, the GUI_DOWNLOAD function module is used to export data from the internal table into a text file stored on the local system. The generated file can then be used by external applications or legacy systems.

In this blog, we will see a simple step-by-step process to:

  • Extract data from an SAP table
  •   Download the data into a text file
  •   Store the file on the desktop using GUI_DOWNLOAD in SAP ABAP

Architecture Overview: 
The following architecture explains how data is transferred from the SAP system into a text file and then utilized by external or legacy systems. 

SAP Database (SAP Tables) 
| 
ABAP Program (SE38 Report) 
| 
Internal Table (Program Data) 
| 
GUI_DOWNLOAD  (Function Module) 
| 
Text/Flat File (.txt) 
| 
Legacy / External Systems

Flow Explanation:

  • The ABAP report reads data from SAP database tables.  
  • The selected data is stored in an internal table.  
  • The GUI_DOWNLOAD function module downloads the data into a text file.  
  • The generated file is stored on the local desktop/system.  
  • External or legacy systems use this file for business operations.

Implementation:
Step 1: Create a file in your local system
Step 2: Right-click on the file and copy the file path. This is my local system path.
             "C:\Users\Lenovo\Desktop\Mara Details.txt"
Step 3: Go to SE38 (T-code). Create a report program.

Step 4: Write the logic, call the GUI_DOWNLOAD function module, and pass the necessary parameters.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& Report ZSAI_RP_SCN_DATA_DOWNLOAD 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
*& 
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------* 
REPORT ZSAI_RP_SCN_DATA_DOWNLOAD. 
 
DATA : lv_file TYPE string. 
START-OF-SELECTION. 
SELECT FROM mara 
  FIELDS matnr, matkl, ersda, mbrsh, ernam 
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_filehandle) 
  UP TO 150 ROWS. 
 
CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD' 
    EXPORTING 
*     BIN_FILESIZE                    = 
      filename                        = 'C:\Users\Lenovo\Desktop\Mara Details.txt' 
*     FILETYPE                        = 'ASC' 
*     APPEND                          = ' ' 
     WRITE_FIELD_SEPARATOR           = 'X' 
    tables 
      data_tab                        = lt_filehandle 
*     FIELDNAMES                      = 
   EXCEPTIONS 
     FILE_WRITE_ERROR                = 1 
     NO_BATCH                        = 2 
     GUI_REFUSE_FILETRANSFER         = 3 
     INVALID_TYPE                    = 4 
     NO_AUTHORITY                    = 5 
     UNKNOWN_ERROR                   = 6 
     HEADER_NOT_ALLOWED              = 7 
     SEPARATOR_NOT_ALLOWED           = 8 
     FILESIZE_NOT_ALLOWED            = 9 
     HEADER_TOO_LONG                 = 10 
     DP_ERROR_CREATE                 = 11 
     DP_ERROR_SEND                   = 12 
     DP_ERROR_WRITE                  = 13 
     UNKNOWN_DP_ERROR                = 14 
     ACCESS_DENIED                   = 15 
     DP_OUT_OF_MEMORY                = 16 
     DISK_FULL                       = 17 
     DP_TIMEOUT                      = 18 
     FILE_NOT_FOUND                  = 19 
     DATAPROVIDER_EXCEPTION          = 20 
     CONTROL_FLUSH_ERROR             = 21 
     OTHERS                          = 22 
            . 
  IF sy-subrc = 0. 
    MESSAGE 'Downloaded Sucessfully' TYPE 'S'. 
  ENDIF.

Step 5: click on execute button

Step 6: After clicking on the Execute button, click on the Allow button. The system will download the data into the specified file.

Step 7: If the data is downloaded successfully into the file, the system displays a message saying "Data saved successfully."

Step 8 : Open the file that you created; inside the file we can see the data.


Conclusion:

In this blog, we learned how to use the GUI_DOWNLOAD function module in SAP ABAP to download SAP data into a text file. We extracted data from an SAP table, stored it in an internal table, and saved it on the local system. This method is useful for sharing SAP data with legacy or external systems through flat files. It is a simple and commonly used technique in SAP ABAP for file handling and data transfer.

entry.title: SAP TM Business Partner Not Created After Transporting Organizational Unit in SAP Transportation Man
entry.content:
Business Partner Not Created After Transporting Organizational Unit in SAP Transportation Management (PPOME)

Problem Overview

When transporting organizational units from a development system to a quality or production system using transaction PPOME in SAP Transportation Management, the associated Business Partner records are not automatically created in the target system. This creates a disconnect between the organizational structure and the required Business Partner master data, potentially disrupting transportation processes that depend on these relationships.

Symptoms

  • Organizational unit successfully transported to target system
  • Business Partner field is populated with development system BP number, but actual Business Partner does not exist in target system
  • No automatic Business Partner creation triggered during transport import
  • Error messages when attempting to use organizational units in TM processes
  • Missing Business Partner roles required for transportation operations 

Root Cause Analysis

This issue occurs due to standard SAP system behavior during transport operations. The transport process copies organizational unit data directly at the database level without triggering the business logic responsible for Business Partner creation.

Enter SE38 (ABAP Editor)
Go to the program RHMOVE30
The first section to fill in, is the Objects Section –
- Plan Version – enter 01 for current plan
- Object Type – enter O for organizational unit
- Object ID – select org by pressing (F4) or clicking the grey circle.
The choose which units are to be transferred, place a tick next to the org units you want to transfer, in this case
Capture the org unit and other details in a transport

Since the BP field is already populated from the source system, the system assumes a Business Partner is already assigned and does not initiate automatic BP creation. Business Partner creation is only triggered during the saving event of organizational unit creation, not during transport import.

Technical Background

In SAP Transportation Management, organizational units and Business Partners have a critical relationship where each organizational unit requires an associated Business Partner for proper functionality. The system establishes a correlation between master data objects Organizational Unit and Business Partner through specific configuration settings. Business Partner integration must be active in the HR area, and proper number range maintenance is essential for successful BP creation.

Resolution Steps

  1. Executing Synchronization Report
    • Run report HRALXSYNC in the target system to create missing Business Partners

    • This report synchronizes Organizational Unit and Business Partner relationships
    • Creates the missing Business Partner records in the target system
  1. Verify HR Integration Settings
    • Check in SM30 V_T77S0 for active HR integration settings
    • Ensure Business Partner integration is activated using SPRO path: Service → Master Data → Business Partner → Integration Business Partner-Organization Management → Set Up Integration with Organizational Management 

 

  1. Validate Number Range Configuration
    • Verify Business Partner number ranges are properly maintained
    • Check organizational unit number range settings
    • Ensure BP grouping configuration is correct 
  2. Check Business Partner Roles
    • Generate required BP role BUP004 if missing
    • Navigate to SPRO → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Basic Settings → Business Partner Roles → Define BP Roles 

Verification Process

  • Confirm Business Partner exists in target system using transaction BP
  • Validate that organizational unit shows correct Business Partner assignment in PPOME
  • Test transportation processes that depend on the organizational unit
  • Verify Business Partner has appropriate roles assigned (Business Partner Gen., Employee, etc.)
  • Check that the system establishes proper correlation between Organizational Unit and Business Partner objects

Prevention Measures

  • Transport Planning: Include Business Partner master data in transport strategy when moving organizational units
  • Configuration Validation: Ensure HR integration and BP integration are properly configured before creating organizational units 
  • Number Range Management: Maintain adequate number ranges for both organizational units and Business Partners 
  • Documentation: Document of the manual synchronization requirement in transport procedures

Related SAP Notes

  • SAP Note 3748231 - Business Partner not created after transporting Organizational Unit in SAP Transportation Management (PPOME) 
  • SAP Note 3340844 - Business Partner is not generated automatically by creating TM Org. Unit via PPOCE 
  • SAP Note 2815138 - Business partner creation failed for organizational unit 
  • SAP Note 3048402 - Transfer Transportation Management Master Data 

Hope that can help you.

Thanks, and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: How to Get Started with Carbon Accounting and Management
entry.content:
Why Carbon Accounting Matters

Organizations worldwide are under increasing pressure to measure, manage, and reduce emissions. Accurate carbon accounting helps businesses understand their environmental impact, comply with regulations, and make informed sustainability decisions.

Carbon accounting and management provide the foundation for:

  • Tracking and reporting greenhouse gas emissions
  • Supporting sustainability and decarbonization strategies
  • Responding to evolving climate regulations
  • Aligning environmental goals with business operations

Understanding these concepts is essential for organizations beginning their sustainability journey.

What You’ll Learn in This Course

This 45-minute free introductory course covers the core principles of carbon accounting and management:

  1. Foundations of Carbon Accounting
    Understand the key concepts, terminology, and processes behind carbon accounting and emissions management.
  2. Climate and Regulatory Context
    Explore the growing importance of carbon accounting in response to climate challenges and regulatory requirements.
  3. Business Relevance
    Learn why carbon accounting matters and how organizations can use it to support sustainability goals and strategic decision-making.

The course also explains SAP’s approach to carbon accounting and how businesses can adapt to future regulatory changes.

Who Should Take the Course?

This course is ideal for:

  • Business Users
  • Consultants 
  • Sales and Presales Professionals

No prerequisites are required, making it a perfect starting point for beginners.

Build a strong foundation in sustainability and emissions management and explore the course “Introducing Carbon Accounting and Management” here.

entry.title: I need help to make Adobe Livecycle Designer 11 work with SAPGui 8.0
entry.content:
Hi 

I have serious trouble installing SAPgui with adobe Livecycle designer for a new PC, so I really hope someone can help me.

I have now tried 3 days in a row and by doing al kind of voodoo, I at first succeded but the next day it has stopped working. 
I have re-implemented multiple times and tried various patch levels of SAPGui.

The problem always occur in SFP when I push the layout button, but it has been different errors every day.
First day SAPGui simply dumped and I had to start SAPLogon again.
Second day I got an error message SOFFICEINTEGRATION 143
And today - third day - I simply get endless hourglass.

Some of the voodoo I have don'e was

  • uninstall the designer and SAP Gui, delete all data in %appdata\Adobe and %appdata%\SAP, Re-install
  • Set compatibility setting to run the designer as administrator
  • In CMD run 2 commands:
    • "C:\Program Files (x86)\Adobe\Designer 11.0\FormDesigner.exe" /unregserver
    • "C:\Program Files (x86)\Adobe\Designer 11.0\FormDesigner.exe" /regserver

After I made it work on day 2 I rebooted my PC and it still worked, but the next morning it was broken again.

My PC is windows 11 pro Version 10.0.26200 Build 26200
Current SAP GUI is 800 patch level 15. I have also tried with 16 and 17.
Current Adobe livecycle designer is version 11.0.9.20260128.1.71
Backend SAP is ECC 6.0 EHP8 
SAP_BASIS 750 SP-level 0034.

Best regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Setting up Document Grounding for Joule with Google Drive
entry.content:
SAP's document grounding service lets you connect Joule to your company's internal documents, so it can answer questions grounded in your actual content rather than relying on general training data.  Since recently Google Drive is available as a data source for Document Grounding, which opens this capability up to organizations running on Google Workspace 🎉 

This post walks through the complete setup enabling Document Grounding for Google Drive: from creating a BTP service instance to configuring a GCP service account, wiring up a BTP destination, and creating an ingestion pipeline that indexes your Google Drive documents. The result is a grounding instance ready to serve vector search queries.

A few things that make Google Drive a practical choice here. There is no agent or connector to install in your Drive environment. Native Google Docs and Google Slides are automatically converted to DOCX and PPTX during ingestion. And if your documents already live in a shared drive or shared folder, you can get grounding running without reorganizing anything.

For the full official documentation, see Set Up Document Grounding on SAP Help Portal.

Note: If you are looking for a setup guide for Microsoft SharePoint instead, I highly recommend visiting the blog from @nageshcaparthy : Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide - 1

What You'll Set Up

Here is what this post covers, in order:

  1. Document Grounding service instance on BTP with Cloud Identity Services authentication
  2. GCP service account with credentials that SAP BTP can use to read your Drive
  3. BTP destination that holds the Google Drive connection configuration
  4. Ingestion pipeline that connects the destination to the grounding service and indexes your documents

The BTP destination holds your GCP credentials. The pipeline references that destination to reach Google Drive. The grounding service processes incoming documents into vector embeddings and stores them for search.

Prerequisites

Before you start, make sure you have:

  • Active Joule instance connected to an IAS tenant in your BTP subaccount (see Establish a Unified Joule Instance)
  • BTP subaccount with document grounding entitlement. Entitlements are added automatically to your global account when you purchase AI Units.
  • GCP Console access with permission to create service accounts and download keys
  • Google Drive folder or shared drive containing the documents you want to index, with permission to share access
  • BTP Cockpit access with permissions to create service instances and destinations in your Joule subaccount
  • curl or an API testing tool (e.g. Bruno) for pipeline creation (there is no BTP Cockpit UI for this step)

Step 1: Create the Document Grounding Service Instance

Create the Service Instance

Open the BTP Cockpit and navigate to your Joule subaccount. Go to Services > Service Marketplace and search for "Document Grounding". Select the tile and choose Create. Set the Runtime Environment (in this case I chose Other), give the instance a name (you will need this name in the next sub-section), and confirm.

Once the instance is created, open it and create a service binding:

 

Give the binding a name such as "docgrounding-binding" and confirm. When the binding is created, open its details and copy the url value. This is the base URL for all document grounding API calls. It could look like this: https://mtls.rage.<id>.kyma.ondemand.com

 

Set Up Authentication

The grounding service API uses bearer token authentication. To get tokens, you create a Cloud Identity Services instance linked to your document grounding instance.

Back in Service Marketplace, search for "Cloud Identity Services" and create a new instance. Set the plan to application, the Runtime Environment (in this case I chose Other), and enter a name.

On the parameters screen, provide the following JSON, substituting your document grounding instance name:

{
  "consumed-services": [
    { "service-instance-name": "<your-doc-grounding-instance-name>" }
  ]
}

This is how it looks like:

 

Once this instance exists, navigate to it via Instances and Subscriptions and create a service binding for it with these parameters:

{
  "credential-type": "X509_GENERATED",
  "validity": 365,
  "validity-type": "DAYS"
}

This generates an X.509 certificate that expires after a year. Open the service binding details and copy these four values: clientid, certificate, key, and authorization_endpoint.

 

Get a Bearer Token

Create two files from the certificate and key values. The JSON stores them with literal \n characters rather than real line breaks, so you need to convert them.

On macOS: Open e.g. a Text Editor, Visual Studio Code or another text tool. Copy this bash command in it:

echo '<certificate-value>' | sed 's/\\n/\n/g' > doc-grounding.crt

Get the copied certificate value from the service binding and add it in the bash command. Open a terminal and provide the full bash command, press enter. This should create a doc-grounding.crt file in the current folder you are in.

Repeat the same step again but use this time the following bash command together with the key value from the service binding.

echo '<key-value>' | sed 's/\\n/\n/g' > doc-grounding.key

On Windows PowerShell: Follow the same instructions as for macOS above with the below PowerShell commands.

[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("doc-grounding.crt", '<certificate-value>'.Replace('\n', "`n"))
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("doc-grounding.key", '<key-value>'.Replace('\n', "`n"))

Edit the authorization_endpoint value from the service binding by replacing /oauth2/authorize at the end with /oauth2/token, like for example https://<ias-tenant-id>.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/token. Enter the clientid value from the service binding. Then request a token using curl or API tools like Bruno. Here is a sample curl request:

curl \
  --request POST \
  --url "<edited-authorization-endpoint>" \
  --header "accept: application/json" \
  --header "content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  --data "client_id=<clientid>" \
  --data "grant_type=client_credentials" \
  --cert doc-grounding.crt \
  --key doc-grounding.key

For the sake of user experience, let's see how it looks like in Bruno:

Start by creating a new collection (e.g. grounding-blog-post) and adding a POST request named "Get Token". Open the collection settings via the ... menu next to the collection name.

Go to the Client Certificates tab in the collection settings. Now we have to add the certificate and the key twice: Once for the IAS tenant domain in order to get the bearer token, and a second time with the document grounding url as domain with the document grounding url to interact with the document grounding service.

Starting with the first client certificate, as Domain the IAS tenant will be used (e.g. https://<ias-tenant-id>.accounts.ondemand.com). Then, set the Cert file to doc-grounding.crt and the Key file to doc-grounding.key. Leave Passphrase empty. Click Add, then Save.

Afterwards, repeat the steps and only change the domain. You can use the authorization_endpoint base url (or url field from document grounding service binding).

Once saved, the configured certificates appear in the list. Repeat the same for the mtls url which is the service-binding-url.

In the request, set the URL to your authorization_endpoint with /oauth2/token at the end. Open the Body tab, select Form URL Encoded, and add the two fields: client_id with your client ID value, and grant_type set to client_credentials.

Send the request. A 200 OK response with an access_token in the body confirms that authentication is working.

Copy the access_token value from the response. You will use this for all subsequent API calls, including pipeline creation in Step 4.

 

Verify the Connection

Test that your service URL and token work together. In Bruno, send a GET request to <service-binding-url>/pipeline/api/v1/pipeline with an Accept: application/json header and your bearer token as the Authorization header.

An empty array [] means the connection works and you have no pipelines yet. That is the expected response at this point.

Step 2: Create a GCP Service Account

The document grounding service needs machine-to-machine access to Google Drive. A service account gives it that without requiring a human user to go through an OAuth consent screen. You share specific Drive content with the service account, and the BTP destination holds the credentials.

Create a GCP Project

If you do not have a GCP project yet, open the GCP Console and create one. Give it a descriptive name like docgrounding-btp-gdrive so its purpose is clear. Note the Project ID — you cannot change it later.

Enable the Google Drive API

Before creating a service account, make sure the Google Drive API is enabled in your GCP project. Navigate to APIs & Services in the GCP Console and click Enable APIs and services.

 

Search for "Google Drive" and select Google Drive API from the results.

Enable it if it is not already active. Without this, service account credentials will not be able to access Drive content even if the sharing is configured correctly.

 

Create the Service Account

Open the GCP Console and navigate to IAM & Admin > Service Accounts. Create a new service account. The name is your choice; something like btp-doc-grounding makes the purpose clear. You do not need to assign any GCP IAM roles to this service account. Access to specific Drive content is granted through Drive sharing, not through IAM roles.

 

Generate a Key

Select your service account, go to the Keys tab, and choose Add Key > Create new key. Select JSON format and download the file. Keep it secure. It contains the private key that BTP will use to authenticate with Google's OAuth token endpoint.

The JSON structure looks like this:

{
  "type": "service_account",
  "project_id": "<your-gcp-project>",
  "private_key_id": "abc123",
  "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n",
  "client_email": "btp-doc-grounding@<your-project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com",
  "client_id": "123456789012342278901",
  "auth_uri": "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth",
  "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token",
  "auth_provider_x509_cert_url": "https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/certs",
  "client_x509_cert_url": "https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/btp-doc-grounding@<your-project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com",
  "universe_domain": "googleapis.com"
}

The fields you will use in the next step:

 

JSON FieldWhere You Will Use It
client_idBTP destination Client ID field
private_keyBTP destination additional property private_key
client_emailBTP destination additional property client_email
auth_uriBTP destination additional property auth_uri

 

Share Your Drive Content with the Service Account

Navigate to the Google Drive folder or shared drive you want to index. For this example we go with a shared folder. Use Share and add the client_email value from the JSON as a member. Set the permission to Viewer. Read access is all the ingestion pipeline needs.

Once shared, you can see the folder with the documents you plan to index.

Here is a sample document in that folder that will be indexed through this pipeline:

While you are in Drive, note the folder ID from the URL. For a shared folder, the URL looks like:

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrSTUvwxYZ
                                       └── this is your folder ID

For a shared drive, open it and grab the ID from the URL after /drive/. You will need this ID when creating the pipeline in Step 4.

Step 3: Configure the BTP Destination

The BTP destination holds your Google Drive connection configuration. The pipeline will reference this destination by name, so the grounding service knows how to authenticate with Google's API.

Open Connectivity > Destinations in your BTP Cockpit and create a new destination with the following main properties:

 

PropertyValue
NameYour choice (e.g. GoogleDrive_DocGrounding)
TypeHTTP
URLhttps://googleapis.com/drive/v3
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID<client_id from service account JSON>
Client SecretLeave empty
Token Service URLhttps://oauth2.googleapis.com/token

Then add these additional properties:

 

PropertyValue
private_keyThe full private_key value from the JSON, including the header and footer lines
client_emailThe client_email from the JSON
auth_urihttps://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth

Save the destination and use Check Connection to verify. A successful response confirms that BTP can reach the Google Drive API using the service account credentials.

Step 4: Create the Ingestion Pipeline

The pipeline tells the grounding service what to index. It connects your BTP destination to a specific Google Drive folder or shared drive and runs the ingestion process. Pipeline creation happens through the grounding service API, not through the BTP Cockpit.

Here is the curl request for a shared folder:

curl \
  --request POST \
  --url "<service-binding-url>/pipeline/api/v1/pipeline" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>" \
  --header "content-type: application/json" \
  --cert doc-grounding.crt \
  --key doc-grounding.key \
  --data '{
    "type": "GoogleDrive",
    "configuration": {
      "destination": "<your-destination-name>",
      "googleDrive": {
        "resourceType": "SHARED_FOLDER",
        "resourceId": "<your-folder-id>",
        "includePaths": ["/**"]
      }
    }
  }'

The response is a JSON object with a pipelineId. Save this ID. You will use it to check ingestion status.

Here is how the request and response look in Bruno (You can copy the data JSON from the curl request above):

Here is what each field does:

  • type: Must be "GoogleDrive" (case-sensitive).
  • destination: The name of the BTP destination you created in Step 3.
  • resourceType: Either SHARED_FOLDER or SHARED_DRIVE. Use the one that matches what you shared with the service account.
  • resourceId: The folder or shared drive ID from the Drive URL.
  • includePaths: Glob patterns for which files to index. ["/**"] indexes everything, but as this parameter is optional, this can be omitted as well. Use ["/policies/**"] to limit to a specific subfolder.

 

SHARED_FOLDER vs SHARED_DRIVE

 

Resource TypeWhen to Use
SHARED_FOLDERYou shared a specific folder within My Drive or a shared drive
SHARED_DRIVEYou shared an entire shared drive

If you leave resourceId empty, the pipeline runs in auto-discovery mode and indexes all shared drives and folders the service account can read. This is convenient for testing but less controlled for production use.

 

Supported File Types

The grounding service ingests these formats from Google Drive:

DOCX, HTML, JPEG, JPG, JSON, PDF, PNG, PPTX, TIFF, TXT.

Despite that, Document Grounding using Google Drive extends the existing support for DOCX and PPTX with processing of native Google Docs and Google Slides.

For more information and availability, see SAP Document Grounding setup documentation.

Verify the Setup

Check Pipeline Status

Use the pipeline ID from the creation response to check ingestion progress:

curl \
  --request GET \
  --url "<service-binding-url>/pipeline/api/v1/pipeline/<pipeline-id>" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>" \
  --cert doc-grounding.crt \
  --key doc-grounding.key

The response includes the pipeline status and a document count. Ingestion for small document sets typically completes within a few minutes. Larger sets take longer.

When ingestion is complete, the status field shows FINISHED.

You can also list the indexed documents by calling <service-binding-url>/pipeline/api/v1/pipeline/<pipeline-id>/executions/<execution-id>/documents. Each indexed document appears with its title, status INDEXED, and a link back to the original Google Drive file.

Note: You first have to get the execution id via this request url: <service-binding-url>/pipeline/api/v1/pipeline/<pipeline-id>/executions

 

Test a Search Query

Now open your Joule Instance that is connected to your IAS. This can be done via Instances and Subscriptions in the BTP Subaccount, then open the Joule instance. Append to your url /webclient/standalone/sap_digital_assistant to open the standalone Joule web client. The full url looks something like that:

https://<tenant>.<region>.sapdas.cloud.sap/webclient/standalone/sap_digital_assistant.

Then provide a prompt that is matching your uploaded document(s).

And here is Joule answering a question about that document's content, with the source citation pointing back to the original file:

And with that, your Google Drive documents are now indexed and searchable through the document grounding service!

Related resources:

  • SAP Help: Set Up Document Grounding
  • SAP Help: Configure Access from SAP BTP
  • SAP Help: Set Up Content Ingestion

entry.title: How to implement Organization Rule to filter out false-positives in SOD Risk Analysis in GRC Access
entry.content:
Organizational Rules in SAP GRC Access Control are specialized filters designed to eliminate false positive segregation of duties (SoD) violations in access risk analysis reports. These rules enable organizations to account for organizational-level restrictions when evaluating access risks, ensuring that conflicts are only reported when they occur within the same organizational boundary (such as company code, cost center, or plant)

Objective:

The primary objective is exception-based reporting to remove false positives that result from organizational-level segregation, where users may have conflicting transactions but are restricted by different organizational values

Scenario / Challenge:

An organization has implemented a shared services model where finance teams are segregated by company code. The current SoD analysis shows false positive conflicts when users have vendor invoice posting rights (FB60) for Company Code 1008 and vendor master data maintenance rights (FK02) for Company Code 1000. While these are conflicting functions, they pose no actual risk since they operate on different organizational entities.

Good Practices for Configuration:

Organizations should implement organizational rules exclusively for exception-based reporting and only after thorough analysis to ensure the situation warrants their use. Rules should not be instituted until the remediation phase of a GRC project, and only after identifying specific organizational rule scenarios.

Critical considerations include:

  • Use organizational rules only when the company has made a conscious decision to segregate via organization levels

  • Avoid using organizational rules for mass organizational level reporting due to performance impact

  • Be cautious not to filter out too much, as it's better to over-report (false positives) than under-report (false negatives) from a control perspective

  • After creating organizational rules, SoD rules must be regenerated using transaction GRAC_GENERATE_RULES

  • Apply the "Consider Org rule" option during risk analysis execution to activate organizational rule filtering

Most companies control data access through role assignment rather than organizational rules, so this functionality should only be enabled for functions requiring specific organizational segregation

Implementation:

Step 1: Identify the specific SoD risk: "Vendor Master Maintenance vs. Process Vendor Invoices"

SOD Setup Diagram:

 

To keep it simple. I have created SOD Risk ‘ZP001_1’ containing two functions ‘ZAP02_1’and ‘ZPR01_1’.

Generate the Risk after creation or modification ( Tcode: GRAC_GENERATE_RULES )

Risk: ZP001_1

 

 

Function: ZAP02_1

 

 

 

Function: ZPR01_1

 

 

Note:

I have added permission which contains BUKRS field as we are considering implementing Organizational Rule for Company Code.

$BUKRS in SAP GRC ruleset permission files is a variable placeholder that represents the Company Code field (BUKRS) in SAP authorization objects

When the ruleset is evaluated, $BUKRS gets populated with actual company code values from the user's authorizations or role assignments

 

Role details:

Role 1: Z_TEST_ROLE_1008

 

Role 2: Z_TEST_ROLE_1000

 

 

Assign this to a test user and run the Repo sync job ( SA38 ->GRAC_REPOSITORY_OBJECT_SYNC )

 

Step 2: Setting up Organization Rule

From the SAP NetWeaver Business Client, navigate to

  • Setup → Under Exception Access Rules → Organization Rules → Create

  • Select the relevant Risk ID for which you want to apply Organizational Rule.

  • Always Generate the Risk after creation or modification of Org Rule. ( Tcode: GRAC_GENERATE_RULES )

 

Step 3: Running Risk Analysis

From the SAP NetWeaver Business Client, navigate to

  • Setup → Under Access Risk Analysis → User Level

  • Input all the relevant Analysis Criteria

 

When we run Risk analysis without considering Org Rule, we see that the values are displayed as $BUKRS which leads to potential false positives

 

 

When we run Risk analysis considering Org Rule, we see that the values are displayed for BUKRS field which helps determine that this risk is clearly false positive and can be ignored.

 

Org Rule ID helps filter out these false positive results and focus on actual risks.

 

Conclusion:

Key Benefits

  1. False Positive Elimination

    • Filters out SoD conflicts that appear risky at the transaction level but are safe due to organizational restrictions

    • Prevents reporting of violations when users have conflicting access across different organizational boundaries (e.g., different company codes)

  2. Precise Risk Tracking and Management

    • Provides unique identification for each organizational rule, enabling targeted application to specific risks

    • Allows granular control over which SoD risks should consider organizational filtering

  3. Enhanced Audit Trail and Compliance

    • Creates a clear reference system for documenting why certain risks were filtered out during analysis

    • Supports regulatory compliance by providing justifiable reasons for risk exceptions based on organizational controls
